macro_events.event_summary: Oil prices edge lower as a fragile Iran ceasefire extension reduces near-term Middle East supply risk premium.
primary_drivers: Lower geopolitical risk premium in crude
Reduced probability of Iranian supply disruption / Strait of Hormuz risk
Lower jet fuel and diesel input costs for transport

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~20-25% of operating costs; a sustained drop in crude from easing Iran risk directly expands margins. Delta has historically been the most fuel-price-sensitive of the legacy carriers given limited hedging.
Similar jet fuel cost structure to DAL, unhedged exposure makes UAL a direct beneficiary of lower crude; also highest beta among legacy carriers.
Southwest has partial hedges but still benefits materially from lower spot jet fuel; domestic-focused carrier sees clean passthrough to margins.
Fuel is a major cost line for the Express air network; lower crude translates directly to lower fuel surcharges net of passthrough lag, boosting near-term margin.
Bunker fuel is one of Carnival's largest variable costs; lower Brent feeds marine fuel prices, expanding cruise operating margin.

losers.rationale: Integrated major with upstream earnings highly levered to Brent; lower crude directly compresses upstream cash flow. Also a bellwether that trades with the tape on oil moves.
Permian-focused E&P with high operating leverage to WTI; unhedged barrels mean near-1:1 earnings sensitivity to crude price declines.
Smaller-cap E&P with higher beta to crude than majors; de-risking of Middle East premium hits names with geopolitical-premium exposure hardest.
Oilfield services demand is tied to operator capex, which is set off crude price expectations; softening oil outlook pressures completions activity sentiment.

headlines: Oil edge lower as fragile Iran ceasefire extension clouds outlook
